content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 5, 1993

Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
The International Building
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, California

RE: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-93-377

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1/ Because your letter does not identify the person
with duties under the Act on whose behalf you are writing, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.2/

Your letter of September 30, 1993, correctly summarizes my
advice. A corporate entity's sponsored statewide general purpose
recipient committee is not required to file campaign disclosure
statements on the same pre-election schedule as the corporate
independent expenditure committee and its sponsored, primarily
formed city ballot measure committee. During 1993, state
general purpose recipient committees are not required to file
regular pre-election statements but may be subject to special
filing requirements. (1992/93 Campaign Disclosure Information
Manual C, pages 19 and 50.)

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5662.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite S00 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: A corporate entity's sponsored statewide general purpose recipient committee is not required to file campaign disclosure statements on the same pre-election schedule as the corporate independent expenditure committee and its sponsored, primarily formed city ballot measure committee. During 1993, state general purpose recipient committees are not required to file regular pre-election statements but may be subject to special filing requirements.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a corporate entity's sponsored statewide general purpose recipient committee must file campaign disclosure statements on the same pre-election schedule as the corporate independent expenditure committee and its sponsored city ballot measure committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A corporate entity's sponsored statewide general purpose recipient committee is not required to follow the same pre-election filing schedule as the independent expenditure committee and city ballot measure committee. State general purpose recipient committees in 1993 are not required to file regular pre-election statements but may have special filing requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; question and conclusion extracted from body text. Document explicitly states this is informal assistance without immunity protections under Government Code Section 83114.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a corporate entity's sponsored statewide general purpose recipient committee must file campaign disclosure statements on the same pre-election schedule as the corporate independent expenditure committee and its sponsored city ballot measure committee.

CONCLUSION: A corporate entity's sponsored statewide general purpose recipient committee is not required to file campaign disclosure statements on the same pre-election schedule as the corporate independent expenditure committee and its sponsored, primarily formed city ballot measure committee. During 1993, state general purpose recipient committees are not required to file regular pre-election statements but may be subject to special filing requirements.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice provided to the requestor regarding campaign disclosure filing requirements for corporate-sponsored recipient committees under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC clarifies that statewide general purpose recipient committees have different filing schedules than independent expenditure committees and may not require regular pre-election statements.
